schema.name: cot_work
schema.description:
Records and tracks a step-by-step plan for the current task. Call this before starting work to make your reasoning explicit, then update it as each step progresses.

When to use:
- The task requires 3 or more distinct actions
- There are phases or dependencies where order matters
- The user asked for more than one thing in a single message
- You generate additional steps while working

When NOT to use:
- Single-step or trivial tasks
- Purely conversational or informational responses

Step management rules:
- Call this first before doing any work — record the full plan upfront
- Always send the complete list on every call, not just changed items
- Exactly one step must be in_progress at any time
- Never jump a step from pending to completed — always set in_progress first
- Mark a step completed immediately when done; do not batch completions
- If scope changes mid-task, update the plan before continuing and set reason
- Do not end your turn until every step is completed

steps.description: The complete current step list. Always include all steps, not just the ones that changed.

task.description: Imperative description of what this step does. e.g. 'Read the target file', 'Apply the patch', 'Run the tests'

status.description: Current status. Transition order: pending -> in_progress -> completed. At most one step may be in_progress at a time.

reason.description: Required only when updating an existing plan mid-task. Explain why the plan changed, e.g. discovered additional work, scope narrowed, earlier step revealed new requirements.
